pattern: Keep a forward map from index to its current number and, per number, a min-ordered set of the indices holding it, skipping indices whose current value no longer matches.
hints: find must return the smallest index for a number quickly, so a linear scan over all indices on every query is too slow.
Maintain two views: what number currently sits at each index, and which indices currently hold a given number.
Use a min-heap (or sorted set) per number of candidate indices; when popping the smallest, confirm that index still maps to that number before accepting it.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: queries before/after fills and a replacement that moves the smallest index
find on a number never inserted returns -1
replacing the value at an index removes it from the old number's candidates
after the last holder of a number is replaced, find returns -1
moving a number back to a smaller index must restore the smaller answer
two numbers tracked independently, lowest index per number
index and number at the constraint extremes

stress.description: interleaved changes (many overwrites) and finds over a small index/number space
stress.note: small index space forces frequent overwrites so stale candidates must be skipped
